Screening: 5 days Occupation: N/A Please refer to the education handout for detailed counseling. EtOH none Smoking status - Former Plan: Counseling - Knee DJD. smoker Musculoskeletal care: I advised the patient that most patients with degenerative joint Quit Smoking: 02/01/2013 disease of the knee can achieve some measure of pain relief from nonsurgical options like medications, lifestyle modification, exercise and physical therapy, and use of supportive devices or change in footwear. The importance of maintaining an ideal body weight or losing weight to reduce stress on the knee joint is a cornestone of nonoperative Tobacco Use management. Does not use vaping products Does not use smokeless Surgical Options and Alternatives tobacco Total knee replacement : I explained to the patient that this is an option for patients that Tobacco Product: None have severe pain in their knee from arthritis who have failed conservative management. The diseased parts of the knee are replaced with metal and plastic. Approximately 90% of patients who have total knee replacement experience a significant reduction in pain and a Advance Care dramatic improvement in their ability to perform common activities of daily living. High Healthcare Proxy: No impact activities should be avoided after surgery, if possible, to prolong the life of the Living Will: No implant. Serious complications occur in less than 2% of patients. Risks include but are not limited to infection, injury to blood vessels and nerves, blood clots, heart complications, lung complications, stroke, and stiffness. Associated with: Order Plain X-ray/Interpretation Follow up. Diagnoses are listed in the order that best reflects the seriousness of each condition and supports the disciplines and services provided. Rate the degree of symptom control for each condition in Column 2. ICD-10-CM sequencing requirements must be followed if multiple coding is indicated for any diagnoses. Code each row according to the following directions for each column: Column 1: Enter the description of the diagnosis. Sequencing of diagnoses should reflect the seriousness of each condition and support the disciplines and services provided. Column 2: Enter the ICD-10-CM code for the condition described in Column 1 no surgical or procedure codes allowed. Codes must be entered at the level of highest specificity and ICD-10-CM coding rules and sequencing requirements must be followed. Note that external cause codes (ICD-10-CM codes beginning with V, W, X, or Y) may not be reported in M1021 (Primary Diagnosis) but may be reported in M1023 (Secondary Diagnoses). Also note that when a Z-code is reported in Column 2 : the code for the underlying condition can often be entered in Column 2, as long as it is an active on-going condition impacting home health care. Rate the degree of symptom control for the condition listed in Column 1. Do not assign a symptom control rating if the diagnosis code is a V, W, X, Y or Z-code. Choose one value that represents the degree of symptom control appropriate for each diagnosis using the following scale: 0 - Asymptomatic, no treatment needed at this time 1 - Symptoms well controlled with current therapy 2 - Symptoms controlled with difficulty, affecting daily functioning; patient needs ongoing monitoring 3 - Symptoms poorly controlled; patient needs frequent adjustment in treatment and dose monitoring 4 - Symptoms poorly controlled; history of re-hospitalizations Note that the rating for symptom control in Column 2 should not be used to determine the sequencing of the diagnoses listed in Column 1. These are separate items and sequencing may not coincide.Insert Diagnosis PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Clinical Group: MS_REHAB)SeverityClear Selection 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 1 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: No_group)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 2 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: Endocrine_3)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 3 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: No_group)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 4 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: Behavioral_2)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 5 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription SeverityClear Selection 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbateInsert Diagnosis(M1028) Active Diagnoses:Comorbidities and Co-existing Conditions Check all that apply See OASIS Guidance Manual for a complete list of relevant ICD-10 codes.1. Peripheral Vascular Disease (PVD) or Peripheral Arterial Disease (PAD) 2. Diabetes Mellitus (DM) 3. None of the above Select or Load H&P PDF from Folder View Detailed Summary QA this OASIS Fill OASIS from H&P Summarize this document List Medications Recommended Diagnosis Reason for admitting to Home Health Recommend Visit Frequency Disclaimer: This AI tool is designed to assist in generating teaching materials and interventions. It should be used as a supplementary resource and not as a substitute for professional medical or educational advice. Users are responsible for reviewing and tailoring the content to meet specific needs and ensure it aligns with current best practices, guidelines, and standards of care.
